title: Dockerfile impossible to build with podman and buildah but working with Docker
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Inexplicable error… Working with Docker but impossible to build using podman/buildah

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. 
```
cat << EOF > Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/centos:8 as builder
RUN useradd -m hsk \
    && rm -rf /var/cache/dnf/* \
    && dnf upgrade -y \
    && dnf install git -y \
    && curl -sSL https://get.haskellstack.org/ | sh \
    && rm -rf /var/cache/dnf/* 

FROM builder
USER hsk
WORKDIR /home/hsk
RUN git clone https://github.com/sycured/streaming_calc_haskell \
    && cd streaming_calc_haskell \
    && stack setup --verbose \
    && stack build --ghc-options='-fPIC -optl-pthread' --test --copy-bins
EOF
```
2. `buildah bud --no-cache --force-rm --squash -f Dockerfile -t test:latest`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
2020-08-23 20:50:20.725850: [debug] Installing library in /home/hsk/.stack/programs/x86_64-linux/ghc-tinfo6-8.6.5/lib/ghc-8.6.5/ghc-prim-0.5.3
2020-08-23 20:51:38.640011: [debug] "utils/ghc-cabal/dist-install/build/tmp/ghc-cabal-bindist" copy libraries/integer-gmp dist-install "strip" '' '/home/hsk/.stack/programs/x86_64-linux/ghc-tinfo6-8.6.5' '/home/hsk/.stack/programs/x86_64-linux/ghc-tinfo6-8.6.5/lib/ghc-8.6.5' '/home/hsk/.stack/programs/x86_64-linux/ghc-tinfo6-8.6.5/share/doc/ghc-8.6.5/html/libraries' 'v p dyn'

Error: Error encountered while installing GHC with

error building at STEP "RUN git clone https://github.com/sycured/streaming_calc_haskell     && cd streaming_calc_haskell     && stack setup --verbose     && stack build --ghc-options='-fPIC -optl-pthread' --test --copy-bins": error while running runtime: exit status 1
ERRO exit status 1                                
```

full log: [error.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/5114994/error.log)


**Describe the results you expected:**

Full success as I obtain with Docker

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Host: VM with CentOS 8 up-to-date

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.4
```

comments.body: Have you tried to build as root?

I just tried as root and … it's working

What's the problem?

I use podman and buildah for the rootless mode and it's not the case if `buildah unshare` isn't sufficient and I need to build as root.

This indicates that most likely there is something in the user namespace that is blocking the build of the container image.  Usually this is UIDs outside of the range or a container within the build doing something the kernel prevents a non root user from doing.

I did `buildah unshare` before to try to build.

Have you any idea to fix it or to give you the right logs?

SELinux was setup at permissive level to avoid any block/kill due to AVC.
@sycured You need to find the maximum UID used within the image, most likely it is larger then 65k.

Have you tried to build the image in privileged mode?

podman build --privileged ...

`Error: unknown flag: --privileged`

```
podman build -
--add-host              --cni-plugin-path       --file                  --layers                --pid                   --security-opt          --userns
--annotation            --cpu-period            --force-rm              -m                      --pull                  --shm-size              --userns-gid-map
--authfile              --cpu-quota             --format                --memory                --pull-always           --squash                --userns-gid-map-group
--build-arg             --cpuset-cpus           -h                      --memory-swap           -q                      --squash-all            --userns-uid-map
--cap-add               --cpuset-mems           --help                  --mount                 --quiet                 -t                      --userns-uid-map-user
--cap-drop              --cpu-shares            --iidfile               --net                   --rm                    --tag                   --uts
--cgroup-parent         --creds                 --ipc                   --network               --runtime               --tls-verify            -v
--cni-config-dir        -f                      --label                 --no-cache              --runtime-flag          --ulimit                --volume
```

Tested with podman 1.6.4 and 2.1.1
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Sorry there is no podman build --privileged.

I just built using your Dockerfile, and it went all the way to completion.
I'll retry with the latest version packaged and I tell you if it works
Have not heard back, so assume it worked.  Reopen if I am mistaken.
